title: Redefining Traditional Models! The U.S. Will Reform Its $200 Billion Research Budget, Shifting the Focus to Individuals and AI
excerpt: In order to accelerate technological upgrades and cope with global competition, the U.S. government plans to make a deep reform in the way federal research fund
content_html:
In order to accelerate technological upgrades and cope with global competition, the U.S. government plans to make a deep reform in the way federal research funds are allocated. The White House Office of Science and Technology Policy recently released a new report, announcing that future funding will no longer overly rely on universities, but instead shift towards individual scientists and artificial intelligence applications.

This new regulation will directly affect the direction of the annual federal R&D budget allocation, which amounts to about 200 billion dollars per year. The White House hopes to directly support scholars with innovative capabilities through personal scholarships and research awards, thus reducing intermediary steps.

Breaking the traditional bureaucratic system, directly funding frontline researchers

For a long time, research funds were mainly allocated to universities first, and then redistributed internally by the schools. This traditional funding model has been criticized for serious bureaucracy, leading to a lot of time and money being wasted on complex administrative tasks.

By directly delivering funds to researchers, the White House aims to break the established approval system that has existed for decades. Although this change may impact large universities that rely on federal funding, decision-makers believe this move can significantly improve the efficiency of scientific research projects.

Putting AI at the core of research, setting multiple cutting-edge technology goals

In addition to changing the funding channels, another key aspect of the new policy is deeply integrating artificial intelligence into all aspects of scientific discovery. Authorities clearly require institutions to regard AI as a new tool that drives breakthroughs, rather than just an ordinary software for daily work.

Along with the shift in funding comes a series of ambitious national technology goals, including deploying powerful quantum computers before 2028 and accelerating the development of energy infrastructure. However, some skeptical scholars have warned that over-reliance on AI technology, which still has flaws, may bring new unknown risks to scientific research.

sentences.zh: 随着资金的转移，一系列雄心勃勃的国家技术目标也随之而来，包括在 2028 年之前部署强大的量子计算机以及加速能源基础设施的发展。

content_html_zh:
为加速技术升级、应对全球竞争，美国政府计划对联邦研究经费分配方式进行深度改革。 白宫科技政策办公室近日发布新报告，宣布未来资金将不再过度依赖大学，而是转向个体科学家和人工智能应用。

这项新规定将直接影响联邦每年约2000亿美元的研发预算分配方向。 白宫希望通过个人奖学金和研究奖项直接支持具有创新能力的学者，从而减少中间步骤。

打破传统官僚体系，直接资助一线科研人员

长期以来，科研经费主要是先分配给大学，然后由学校内部再分配。 这种传统的资助模式因严重的官僚主义而受到批评，导致大量时间和金钱浪费在复杂的行政任务上。

通过直接向研究人员提供资金，白宫旨在打破已经存在了数十年的既定审批制度。 尽管这一变化可能会影响依赖联邦资助的大型大学，但决策者认为此举可以显着提高科研项目的效率。

以人工智能为研究核心，设定多项前沿技术目标

除了改变资助渠道外，新政策的另一个关键方面是将人工智能深度融入科学发现的各个方面。 当局明确要求机构将人工智能视为推动突破的新工具，而不仅仅是日常工作的普通软件。

随着资金的转移，一系列雄心勃勃的国家技术目标也随之而来，包括在 2028 年之前部署强大的量子计算机以及加速能源基础设施的发展。 不过，一些持怀疑态度的学者警告称，过度依赖仍存在缺陷的人工智能技术可能会给科学研究带来新的未知风险。
